text: The laparoscopic view reveals small bowel protruding through from underneath the Roux limb of the gastric bypass, indicating an internal hernia.

The surgeon identifies the anatomical structures involved in the internal hernia, focusing on the Peterson defect located between the mesentery of the large bowel, the mesentery of the small bowel, and the retroperitoneum. The hernia is visually reduced during this step.
After reducing the small bowel from the hernia, the surgeon uses an endoscopic suturing device to close the Peterson defect with a braided permanent suture.
The surgeon initiates a purse-string suture at the apex of the hernia defect and continues with a continuous running suture extending up to the border of the transverse colon.
